title: विष्णु षोडशोपचार पूजा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु षोडशोपचार पूजा मन्त्राः ॥

ध्यानम्—
शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं
विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम्।
लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं
वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम्॥

१। आवाहनम्—
सहस्रशीर्षा पुरुषः सहस्राक्षः सहस्रपात्।
आगच्छ देवदेवेश मम पूजां गृहाण भोः॥
ॐ श्री विष्णवे नमः। आवाहयामि॥

२। आसनम्—
रत्नसिंहासनं चारु सुवर्णाढ्यं मनोहरम्।
आसनं कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमः॥

३। पाद्यम्—
गंगादि सर्वतीर्थेभ्यः समानीतं जलं शुभम्।
पाद्यं गृहाण देवेश विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

४। अर्घ्यम्—
सुगन्धिपुष्पसंयुक्तं चन्दनागरुभूषितम्।
अर्घ्यं गृहाण विश्वेश विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

५। आचमनीयम्—
कर्पूरेलालवंगादि सुगन्धिजलसंयुतम्।
आचमनीयं गृहाण विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

६। स्नानम्—
मन्दाकिनीजलैर्दिव्यैः पञ्चामृतसमन्वितैः।
स्नानं करोमि देवेश विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

७। वस्त्रम्—
पीताम्बरं सुवर्णाढ्यं दिव्यं सूक्ष्मं मनोहरम्।
वस्त्रयुग्मं प्रदास्यामि विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

८। यज्ञोपवीतम्—
राजतं ब्रह्मसूत्रं च कांचनं च मनोहरम्।
yajnopavītaṃ दास्यामि विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

९। गन्धम्—
श्रीखण्डं चन्दनं दिव्यं गन्धाढ्यं सुमनोहरम्।
विलेपनं कल्पयामि विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

१०। पुष्पम्—
माल्यादीनि सुगन्धीनि तुलसीदलानि च।
puṣpāṇi कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

११। धूपम्—
वनस्पतिरसोद्भूतो गन्धाढ्यो गन्ध उत्तमः।
धूपं कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

१२। दीपम्—
घृतवर्तिसमायुक्तं अन्धकारविनाशनम्।
दीपं कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

१३। नैवेद्यम्—
षड्रसोपेतं नैवेद्यं पायसापूपसंयुतम्।
नैवेद्यं कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

१४। ताम्बूलम्—
पूगीफलसमायुक्तं नागवल्लीदलैर्युतम्।
ताम्बूलं कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

१५। दक्षिणा—
हिरण्यगर्भगर्भस्थं हेमबीजं विभावसोः।
दक्षिणां कल्पयामि श्री विष्णवे नमो नमः॥

१६। मन्त्रपुष्पम्—
ॐ नमो नारायणाय विद्महे वासुदेवाय धीमहि।
तन्नो विष्णुः प्रचोदयात्।
नमस्ते देवदेवेश नमस्ते धरणीधर।
नमस्ते सर्वलोकेश प्रदक्षिणनमस्कारम्॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु षोडशोपचार पूजा मन्त्राः सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja Mantrah ||

Dhyanam—
Shantakaram bhujaga-shayanam padmanabham suresham
Vishvadharam gagana-sadrisham megha-varnam shubhangam |
Lakshmi-kantam kamala-nayanam yogibhir-dhyana-gamyam
Vande Vishnum bhava-bhaya-haram sarva-lokaika-natham ||

1. Avahanam—
Sahasra-shirsha purushah sahasrakshah sahasrapat |
Agachchha deva-devesha mama pujam grihana bhoh ||
Om Shri Vishnave Namah. Avahayami ||

2. Asanam—
Ratna-simhasanam charu suvarna-adhyam manoharam |
Asanam kalpayami Shri Vishnave Namah ||

3. Padyam—
Gangadi sarva-tirthebhyah samanitam jalam shubham |
Padyam grihana devesha Vishnave namo namah ||

4. Arghyam—
Sugandhi-pushpa-samyuktam chandana-agaru-bhushitam |
Arghyam grihana vishvesha Vishnave namo namah ||

5. Achamaniyam—
Karpurelala-vangadi sugandhi-jala-samyutam |
Achamaniyam grihana Vishnave namo namah ||

6. Snanam—
Mandakini-jalair-divyaih panchamrita-samanvitaih |
Snanam karomi devesha Vishnave namo namah ||

7. Vastram—
Pitambaram suvarnadhyam divyam sukshmam manoharam |
Vastra-yugmam pradasayami Vishnave namo namah ||

8. Yajnopavitam—
Rajatam brahma-sutram cha kanchanam cha manoharam |
Yajnopavitam dasyami Vishnave namo namah ||

9. Gandham—
Shrikhandam chandanam divyam gandhadhyam sumanoharam |
Vilepanam kalpayami Vishnave namo namah ||

10. Pushpam—
Malyadini sugandhini tulasi-dalani cha |
Pushpani kalpayami Shri Vishnave namo namah ||

11. Dhupam—
Vanaspati-rasodbhuto gandhadhyo gandha uttamah |
Dhupam kalpayami Shri Vishnave namo namah ||

12. Dipam—
Ghrita-varti-samayuktam andhakara-vinashanam |
Dipam kalpayami Shri Vishnave namo namah ||

13. Naivedyam—
Shadrasopetam naivedyam payasapupa-samyutam |
Naivedyam kalpayami Shri Vishnave namo namah ||

14. Tambulam—
Pugi-phala-samayuktam nagavalli-dalair-yutam |
Tambulam kalpayami Shri Vishnave namo namah ||

15. Dakshina—
Hiranyagarbha-garbhastham hema-bijam vibhavasoh |
Dakshinam kalpayami Shri Vishnave namo namah ||

16. Mantrapushpam—
Om namo Narayanaya vidmahe Vasudevaya dhimahi |
Tanno Vishnuh prachodayat |
Namaste deva-devesha namaste dharani-dhara |
Namaste sarva-lokesha pradakshina-namaskaram ||

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja Mantrah Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान— शान्त आकार वाले, शेषनाग पर शयन करने वाले, पद्मनाभ, सुरों के ईश्वर, विश्व के आधार, आकाश के समान, मेघ वर्ण, शुभ अंगों वाले, लक्ष्मीकान्त, कमलनयन, योगियों द्वारा ध्यान में प्राप्य, भवभय को हरने वाले, सर्वलोकों के एकमात्र नाथ विष्णु को प्रणाम।

१. आवाहन— सहस्र सिर, सहस्र नेत्र, सहस्र पाद वाले पुरुष, हे देवदेवेश, आइये, मेरी पूजा स्वीकार कीजिये।
२. आसन— रत्न जड़ित सुवर्ण सिंहासन अर्पित करता हूँ।
३. पाद्य— गंगा आदि सभी तीर्थों से लाया हुआ शुभ जल चरण धोने के लिए अर्पित।
४. अर्घ्य— सुगन्धित पुष्प, चन्दन व अगरु से सुशोभित अर्घ्य अर्पित।
५. आचमनीय— कपूर, इलायची, लवंग आदि सुगन्धित जल आचमन के लिए अर्पित।
६. स्नान— मन्दाकिनी के दिव्य जल और पञ्चामृत से स्नान कराता हूँ।
७. वस्त्र— दिव्य पीताम्बर वस्त्र अर्पित।
८. यज्ञोपवीत— सुवर्ण ब्रह्मसूत्र अर्पित।
९. गन्ध— दिव्य श्रीखण्ड चन्दन का लेप अर्पित।
१०. पुष्प— सुगन्धित माला और तुलसी दल अर्पित।
११. धूप— वनस्पति रस से निर्मित उत्तम सुगन्धित धूप अर्पित।
१२. दीप— घृत की बाती वाला अन्धकार नाशक दीप अर्पित।
१३. नैवेद्य— षड्रस युक्त, पायस और पूआ सहित नैवेद्य अर्पित।
१४. ताम्बूल— सुपारी और नागवल्ली पत्र सहित ताम्बूल अर्पित।
१५. दक्षिणा— हिरण्यगर्भ के स्वर्ण बीज रूप दक्षिणा अर्पित।
१६. मन्त्रपुष्प— विष्णु गायत्री मन्त्र सहित प्रदक्षिणा और नमस्कार।
benefits: Completes the sixteen-step ritual worship of Lord Vishnu
Invokes the blessing of immense merit equivalent to elaborate temple puja
Purifies the home and environment with divine vibrations
Invokes fulfillment of all wishes when performed with devotion
Traditionally associated with peace, prosperity, and divine protection
Ideal for daily worship and special occasions
description: Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja — विष्णु षोडशोपचार पूजा — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Vaishnavite Agama Tradition, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to bring peace, prosperity, and divine protection, purify the home and environment with divine vibrations, and bestow immense merit equivalent to elaborate temple puja. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Ekadashi and Kartik Purnima for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 16 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja (विष्णु षोडशोपचार पूजा) is the classical sixteen-step (shodasha = sixteen, upachara = service or offering) ritual worship paddhati of Lord Vishnu, drawn from the Vaishnavite Pancharatra and Vaikhanasa Agama traditions and codified in numerous Smriti texts and puja-paddhati manuals. The sixteen sequential upacharas (services) — beginning with Avahanam (invocation) and ending with Mantrapushpam (mantra-flower offering) — together constitute a complete temple-grade puja that can be performed at home, replicating the elaborate ritual worship offered at major Vishnu temples. The complete sequence is: (1) Dhyanam (meditation) and (2-17) the sixteen upacharas: Avahanam, Asanam, Padyam, Arghyam, Achamaniyam, Snanam, Vastram, Yajnopavitam, Gandham, Pushpam, Dhupam, Dipam, Naivedyam, Tambulam, Dakshina, Mantrapushpam. (Sources: Vaikhanasa Bhagavad-archana Adhikara; Pancharatra Agama; Smarta puja paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning and purpose of the dhyana shloka Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The puja opens with the universal Vishnu Dhyana shloka — Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanabham Suresham | Vishvadharam Gagana-sadrisham Megha-varnam Shubhangam | Lakshmi-kantam Kamala-nayanam Yogibhir-dhyana-gamyam | Vande Vishnum Bhava-bhaya-haram Sarva-loka-eka-natham — meaning 'I bow to Vishnu, the remover of fear of samsara, the one Lord of all the worlds — He of peaceful form (shantakara), reclining on the serpent (bhujaga-shayanam = Sheshanaga), the lotus-naveled (Padmanabham), the lord of devas (suresha), supporter of the universe (vishvadhara), vast as the sky, dark like a rain-cloud, with auspicious limbs, the beloved of Lakshmi, lotus-eyed, attainable by yogis through meditation.' This dhyana fixes the deity's iconic form (rupa-dhyana) in the puja-karta's mind before invoking Him into the murti or yantra. (Source: classical Vishnu dhyana shloka; Pancharatra Agama paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the sixteen upacharas (services) in shodashopachara puja?
seoFaqs.answer: The sixteen upacharas are: (1) Avahanam — invocation of the deity into the murti / kalasha; (2) Asanam — offering a seat (usually a ratna-simhasana, gem-throne); (3) Padyam — water for washing the lord's feet; (4) Arghyam — water with flowers and chandana for honoured offering; (5) Achamaniyam — water with cardamom, clove, camphor for sipping; (6) Snanam — bath, often pancha-amrita-snana (milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar) followed by water; (7) Vastram — offering of fresh garments (pitambara, yellow silk for Vishnu); (8) Yajnopavitam — offering the sacred thread; (9) Gandham — sandalwood paste / chandana anointment; (10) Pushpam — flowers and tulasi-dala (essential for Vishnu); (11) Dhupam — incense offering; (12) Dipam — lighted lamp (ghrita-dipa); (13) Naivedyam — food offering (shadrasa — six tastes); (14) Tambulam — betel leaf with arecanut; (15) Dakshina — offering of gold or money; (16) Mantrapushpam — flower offered with the Vishnu Gayatri mantra, followed by pradakshina and namaskara. (Source: Vaikhanasa Bhagavad-archana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Tulasi essential in Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: Tulasi (Holy Basil, Ocimum sanctum) is the most beloved offering to Lord Vishnu — He is called Tulasi-priya and Tulasi-vana-vihari. The Padma Purana and Brahma Vaivarta Purana narrate that Tulasi was originally Vrinda, a great devotee of Vishnu whose pativrata-dharma was protecting her demon-husband Jalandhara; when Vishnu broke her vow to enable Shiva to slay Jalandhara, He blessed her to be His eternally-beloved consort in the form of the Tulasi plant, never to be separated from His worship. Hence: (1) every Vishnu puja must include Tulasi-dala; (2) a Vishnu naivedyam without Tulasi is not accepted; (3) the bilva-patra of Shiva is replaced by tulasi-dala for Vishnu; (4) the Tulasi-vivaha festival in Kartik month celebrates her wedding to Vishnu; (5) Tulasi is grown in every Vaishnava household courtyard. The Pushpam upachara (step 10) specifically includes Tulasi-dala alongside other flowers. (Sources: Padma Purana Tulasi Mahatmya; Brahma Vaivarta Purana; Hari Bhakti Vilasa.)

seoFaqs.question: Can householders perform Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja at home?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — householder Vishnu shodashopachara puja is one of the most highly-recommended grihastha-dharma practices and is accessible to any devotee with basic puja-samagri (worship articles): a Vishnu murti or shaligrama-shila or tulsi-mandapa or simple framed picture; a small puja-thali with kalasha (water-pot), achamana-patra, panchapatra, kumkum, chandana, tulasi-dala, flowers, akshata, dhupa-batti, ghee-deepa, naivedyam (any sweet, fruit or cooked food), tambulam, dakshina coin, and a clean asana for the puja-karta. The puja takes 15-30 minutes when performed simply, and 1-3 hours when performed elaborately with full snana, abhisheka and mantra-archana. Many householders do an abbreviated panchopachara (5-step) or dashopachara (10-step) on regular days and the full shodashopachara on Ekadashi, Vishnu festivals and Kartik Purnima. (Sources: classical grihastha-dharma; Vaishnava puja paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja be performed?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Shodashopachara Puja is most powerful on Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu's day), Saturday (Shanivar — the day of Saturn, pacified through Vishnu worship), every Ekadashi (Shukla and Krishna Paksha — the supreme Vishnu day), Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha-Pausha Shukla Ekadashi, the year's supreme Vishnu festival), Anant Chaturdashi (Bhadrapada Shukla Chaturdashi), Kartik Purnima (the day Vishnu emerged from chaturmasya yogic sleep), Devotthana Ekadashi (Kartik Shukla Ekadashi — when Vishnu wakes from yoga-nidra), Tulasi Vivaha day (Kartik Shukla Dwadashi), Janmashtami (for Krishna form), Ram Navami (for Rama form), Narasimha Jayanti (for Narasimha form), and during the four Chaturmasya months (Ashadha-Ashvin) when Vishnu is in cosmic sleep. The morning Brahma-muhurta (4-6 am) is the ideal time, followed by sunrise. (Sources: Vaishnava festival calendar; Drikpanchang Vaikuntha Ekadashi observances.)
